marketState: neutral, fundamentals firming (hot weather, rising fair value) vs bearish technical trend and storage surplus
summary: Price ($2.884) sits almost exactly at fair value ($2.923, FAIR status) but the fair-value curve climbs to $3.080 by +21D, and spot is running 8.4pp below the seasonal median YTD, both arguing for upside drift if fundamentals hold. Weather is the standout signal: demand is +21.6% vs 5y in days 1-7 and +20.2% vs 5y in days 8-16 with range position near the top of its band (0.99), a genuinely hot forecast that should support summer cooling burn. Working against that: storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise this week, managed money is moderately net short, and technicals are bearish across the 15m-1w timeframes (50<200 EMA cross, negative daily/weekly MACD) even though the shortest timeframes look oversold (5m RSI 28.9, below lower Bollinger). Seasonality itself is a weak signal here - reliability is explicitly "Low" with a 59pp spread and only a 38% historical probability of higher prices 3-4 weeks out. Net read: a fundamentally-supported but technically-fragile market, better suited to a patient long-term long than a short-term directional bet right now.

longTerm.riskReward: approx 1:1.7 blended (1:0.7 to TP1, 1:2.5 to TP2)
longTerm.rationale: Demand is running 20%+ above 5-year norms in both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows (rangePos 0.84 and 0.99), and the fair-value curve rises from $2.923 now to $3.080 by +21D while spot is 8.4pp below the seasonal median YTD - both point to upward pull over a multi-week horizon, with September historically the strongest month. Moderate managed-money net-short positioning (35th 5y percentile) leaves room for short-covering if the heat persists.
longTerm.invalidation: A close below $2.55 (below the rising fair-value band's lower edge) or weather forecasts reverting to near-normal in the 8-16D window would break this thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term weather (+21.6% demand vs 5y) is bullish and price is sitting right at fair value (spread -$0.039, FAIR), but every technical timeframe from 15m to 1w is bearish (50<200 EMA cross, negative daily/weekly MACD) while the shortest frames are already oversold (5m RSI 28.9, below lower Bollinger) and today's storage print was a bearish surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast). These signals conflict directly, so there is no clean edge for a 1-10 day trade right now.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a reclaim of $2.95-$3.00 with daily MACD turning positive to enter long, or (b) a confirmed break and close below $2.75 with RSI staying under 30 to enter short.
